name: Abdullah ibn Yaqtar
title: Foster Brother
shortDescription: Imam Hussain's (AS) foster brother who was sent to Kufa as an ambassador and was martyred by Ibn Ziyad.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Yaqtar was a sincere Shia and the foster brother of Imam Hussain (AS). His mother had breastfed Imam Hussain (AS) in infancy, which is why he is called the Imam's foster brother.

When Imam Hussain (AS) departed from Mecca towards Kufa, he sent Abdullah with a letter to Muslim ibn Aqeel in Kufa to learn about the situation. However, he was arrested on the way by the forces of Husayn ibn Numayr (Ibn Ziyad's agent).

Ibn Ziyad took him to Kufa and demanded that he curse Imam Hussain (AS) in front of the people. With unparalleled bravery, Abdullah ibn Yaqtar went to the top of the Dar al-Imara, but instead of cursing the Imam, he cursed Yazid and Ibn Ziyad and urged the people to rush to Imam Hussain's (AS) aid.

Enraged, Ibn Ziyad ordered him to be thrown from the roof of the Dar al-Imara. He fell to the ground with a crushed body but still had a breath of life when a person (Abd al-Malik ibn Umayr al-Lakhmi) attacked him and severed his head, leading to his martyrdom.
quoteArabic: أَيُّهَا النّاسُ! أَنا رَسُولُ الحُسَيْنِ ابْنِ فاطِمَةَ...
quote: (His cry from the Dar al-Imara roof:) O people! I am the messenger of Hussain, son of Fatima, to you. Help him...
